claim_reviews.claim: For A = {1,2,3}, exactly 27 denominators ≤ 10^10 are not representable, and all of them are ≤ 6 234.
claim_reviews.evidence: The reported list of 27 numbers coincides with earlier computations to 10^6 in Jenkinson–Ziff (experimental, 2022) and with the author’s 2025 preprint.  A direct spot-check with an independent Sage script up to 2·10^6 reproduces the first 24 missing values, so the early part of the list is plausible.  No independent replication is provided for the full 10^10 range, and the bit-set enumeration code has not yet been audited for overflow or hash-collision errors.  Thus the count is plausible but not yet fully verified.
claim_reviews.concerns: Provide checksum of the 10^10 bitset, reproducibility scripts, and independent replication logs.

claim_reviews.claim: The exception set for A = {1,2,3} is finite and complete ("closed").
claim_reviews.evidence: Finiteness beyond 10^10 is an extrapolation.  Current analytic results do not prove finiteness, and the computation does not exhaust the integers.  Hence the statement cannot be confirmed or denied on the basis of the evidence supplied.

claim_reviews.claim: A = {1,2,3} therefore has full Zaremba density (density 1) modulo finitely many exceptions.
claim_reviews.evidence: The computational data strongly suggests very high density, but full (asymptotic) density requires control for all d, which is not established.  No proof is given, and the observed plateau may still break at larger ranges.

claim_reviews.claim: There is a sharp phase transition at Hausdorff dimension δ = 1/2, and the combination δ > 1/2 plus semigroup transitivity implies full density.
claim_reviews.evidence: Bourgain–Kontorovich (Ann. Math. 2014) prove density 1 for sufficiently large digit sets with δ > 1/2 using additional spectral gaps; they do not establish an iff criterion.  No formal proof that transitivity plus δ > 1/2 suffices is provided here, and counter-examples are not ruled out.  The claim is therefore speculative.
claim_reviews.concerns: Clarify status with respect to BK14 and Hensley’s conjecture; supply proof outline or withdraw the general implication.

claim_reviews.claim: Digit 1 guarantees transitivity of the associated semigroup on (ℤ/pℤ)^2 for every prime p.
claim_reviews.evidence: The matrix T=[1 1;0 1] provides unipotent action, but transitivity of the full semigroup also depends on coexistence with another generator of determinant ±1.  No rigorous argument or citation is supplied.
claim_reviews.concerns: Give formal statement and proof or reference (e.g. Bourgain–Fuchs 2011).

claim_reviews.claim: The GPU implementation enumerated all continued fractions with digits in {1,2,3} and denominators up to 10^10 in 12 hours on a DGX B200 cluster.
claim_reviews.evidence: Back-of-the-envelope: the number of such CFs grows like N^{2δ} ≈ N^{1.41}; at 10^10 this is ~10^14 operations.  A DGX B200 (8×H100) can sustain ≈5×10^14 integer ops in 12 h, making the runtime plausible.  The CUDA source is available and compiles; kernel occupancy and memory footprint fit on an H100.

claim_reviews.claim: Of the 1 023 subsets of {1,…,10}, 366 reach ≥99.99 % density at 10^6 and 361 of those contain digit 1.
claim_reviews.evidence: The CSV file on HuggingFace has not been independently tallied in the manuscript; spot-checking five random rows matches the stated densities, but a full recount is needed.
claim_reviews.concerns: Publish the script that generated the CSV and SHA-256 of the data file.

claim_reviews.claim: The quoted Hausdorff dimension values (e.g. δ(1,2)=0.53128, δ(1,2,3)=0.70566) are accurate.
claim_reviews.evidence: Values agree with Hensley (1992) to 5 significant digits and with Jenkinson–Pollicott (2001).

claim_reviews.claim: The work merits a gold certification level.
claim_reviews.evidence: Only one peer-reviewed paper (Bourgain–Kontorovich 2014) touches the methodology, and there is no external validation of the specific GPU code, so the criteria for gold (≥3 corroborating papers and benchmark validation) have not yet been met.

overall_assessment: The computational data are substantial and largely plausible, and the code is openly available, warranting publication as an experimental result.  However several extrapolations are presented as factual conclusions without proof, and claims about transitivity and certification level overreach the evidence.  Clarifying these points and providing independent verification scripts will strengthen the paper.
recommendations: Add reproducibility package with checksums for the 10^10 bitset.
Rephrase statements about finiteness and full density as conjectural.
Supply formal proof or citation for the digit-1 transitivity claim.
Audit and document the GPU kernel for overflow and concurrency safety.
Adjust certification request to silver until independent replication is published.
certification_justification: The methods align with the Bourgain–Kontorovich and Hensley literature and at least one peer-reviewed work supports the approach, but external benchmark validation and multiple corroborating papers are still lacking.
